mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held below prior-month levels, discount rates decline and equity valuations expand, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts occur alongside accelerating inflation that forces a risk-off repricing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with flat-to-lower FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase above the recent trend signals inflation pressure that raises expected policy tightening and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails when inflation rises but FEDFUNDS is already easing aggressively.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if CPI cross-above-trend episodes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal has negative t-statistic over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand slowdown and softer corporate revenue expectations, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires both retail sales and housing to deteriorate together rather than only one series weakening.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if either series alone explains the signal better than the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates labor market deterioration and weaker future earnings, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if unemployment rises only after equity drawdowns are already underway.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold events are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal's lead-lag relation disappears after controlling for prior equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both improve, it signals expanding production capacity and payroll income, supporting higher earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if industrial output rises while labor growth contracts, indicating inventory restocking without true demand.
failure_condition: Reject if joint monthly gains in INDPRO and PAYEMS are not associated with positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the pair adds no predictive value beyond INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after prior weakness signals improving aggregate demand and future earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when GDP growth improves only because of transitory inventory effects without sustained demand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 grows faster than its prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 acceleration does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening regime, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL should depress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is specifically a policy-pressure mechanism and fails when rates rise but inflation is clearly easing.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS/high-CPI episodes do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the interaction term is not stronger than each series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth stays strong while UNRATE remains low, income and consumption support corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails in late-cycle labor overheating, where strong payrolls coincide with inflation pressure and the market sells off.
failure_condition: Reject if strong-payroll/low-unemployment states do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the relationship reverses during low-volatility periods.
